According to the ABA Model Rules of Professional Conduct, when lawyers from different law firms are jointly representing a client, they have an obligation to consult with each other and the client about the scope of their respective representations. This is done to ensure that the client's interests are not divided between the lawyers and to avoid conflicts of interest. If they fail to do so, they run the risk of violating their duty of loyalty to the client. ###
